Portfolio — Electrical & Electronics Engineering

Abhishek Kishor .

Electrical and Electronics Engineer (VIT Chennai) working across motor design, renewable energy, and IoT systems — with published research in premium-efficiency induction motors and smart sustainable homes.

See the projects Get in touch
01

About

I'm an electrical and electronics engineer with a focus on motor design and energy efficiency, currently working through problems where electromagnetics, thermal analysis, and real-world manufacturing standards meet. My final-year project redesigned 1.1kW–30kW induction motors to meet IE3, IE4, and IE5 efficiency classes, work that later became a published paper in the IEEE Canadian Journal of Electrical and Computer Engineering.

Alongside motor design, I've built IoT and embedded systems — from a solar-integrated EV range study to a smart sewage monitoring framework for sustainable homes. I like projects that sit at the intersection of hardware, simulation, and measurable efficiency gains.

Based inIndia

02

Education

2019 — 2023

B.Tech, Electrical and Electronics Engineering

VIT (Vellore Institute of Technology), Chennai, India

Jun 2022 — Apr 2023

Final Year Project — Premium-Efficiency Induction Motor Design

Redesigned 1.1kW–30kW induction motors for IE3–IE5 efficiency standards, compliant with Siemens Low Voltage specifications. Ran detailed FEA and thermal analysis in Simcenter Motorsolve and SolidWorks to validate the designs.

03

Projects

2024 – 2025

Solar Electric Vehicle

SolidWorks Rhino 3D Renewable Energy

Designed and validated a solar-integrated Tata Nexon EV, using SolidWorks, Rhino 3D, and GMT weather data to optimize energy harvesting and quantify how much driving range solar assistance could add.

2023

IoT Weather Station

ESP8266 MATLAB ThingSpeak 3D Printing

Developed and deployed an IoT-based weather monitoring system with custom 3D-printed sensor housings, integrated via ESP8266 and MATLAB ThingSpeak for real-time data visualization.

2022 – 2023

Electromagnetic & Thermal Analysis of Induction Motors

Simcenter Motorsolve SolidWorks FEA

Designed and validated 1.1kW–30kW induction motors to meet IE3–IE5 efficiency standards, using finite element and thermal analysis to guide optimization decisions.

2021 – 2022

Solar / Grid Failover System

Mobile Control SoC Switching

Built a mobile-controlled system that switches between solar battery and grid power based on state of charge, with automatic failover to cut grid dependency and maximize renewable use.

2020 – 2021

IoT-Based Smart Wet Grinder

NodeMCU ESP8266 Blynk LM35

Developed an automated, remote-controlled motor system with an LM35-based thermal cutoff, improving safety and operational efficiency. Presented at the 2021 International Conference of Undergraduate Students.

04

Publications

Design and Simulation of Four-Pole Induction Motors for Premium, Super-Premium, and Ultra-Premium Efficiency

Since Indian electric power use leans heavily on agriculture, where induction motors run as pumps, the paper looks at moving beyond today's standard IE3 motors toward the more efficient IE4 (super-premium) and IE5 (ultra-premium) classes. It works through the design of all three efficiency tiers with detailed electromagnetic and thermal analysis, aiming to give researchers and industry designers a practical path to more energy-efficient motors.

Abhishek Kishor, Natesan Chokkalingam Lenin, Sathyanarayanan Nandagopal, Arjun Seshadri, A. Bharathi Sankar Ammaiyappan, Mohamed N. Ibrahim — IEEE Canadian Journal of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Vol. 49, Issue 2, Spring 2026, pp. 118–129. Published 18 February 2026. DOI: 10.1109/ICJECE.2026.3652824.

View paper →

IoT-Based Sustainable Homes Aided with the Smart Sewage Monitoring System

Book chapter arguing for solar-equipped smart homes as a way to ease pressure on conventional power grids as smart-home adoption keeps climbing. Rooftop solar charges a battery that's injected into the grid when full, and the homeowner can monitor battery state of charge and switch the house onto stored solar power during outages via a mobile app — automatically reconnecting to the grid when the battery runs low. The same framework folds in a smart sewage monitoring system that tracks wastewater levels and flow in real time, as part of the "Digital Cities" smart-city research collection.

V. Ananthakrishnan, Dixshant Kumar Jha, Abhishek Kishor, Ashutosh Dwivedi, Jeet Tiwari, Tritoy Mohanty, Aryan Dash, P. Balamurugan, Sitharthan Ramachandran, O.V. Gnana Swathika — Book Editors: O.V. Gnana Swathika, K. Karthikeyan. Wiley Online Library, Chapter 6, first published 29 September 2025. DOI: 10.1002/9781394233823.ch6.

View chapter →

IoT Based Smart Wet Grinder

Paper on integrating IoT with a household wet grinder — a widely used appliance in India for grinding batter, rice, and dhal. Motivated by diagnostic data showing roughly 100,000 wet grinders produced annually in India (Ministry of MSME study, Coimbatore), the work retrofits a Ponmani Power Table Top Tilting Wet Grinder (180W induction motor, 180–240V, 50Hz) with IoT features: remote power control, an automated water pump for batter consistency, motor-temperature monitoring with automatic cutoff, and a timer for scheduled on/off control. A 2-channel relay driven by a NodeMCU ESP8266 handles the grinder and pump, with an LM35 sensor tracking motor temperature, and the whole system is controlled remotely through the Blynk app.

R. Kishore, Shini Gupta, Abhishek Kishor, Arshad Iqbal, Sriramalakshmi P — In: Prashant Singh Rana, Deepak Bhatia, Himanshu Arora (eds), SCRS Proceedings of International Conference of Undergraduate Students, SCRS, India, 2023, pp. 177–184. ISBN: 978-81-95502-01-1. DOI: 10.52458/978-81-95502-01-1-20.

View paper →
